reasoning: Chegg is fundamentally distressed due to AI challenges, with revenue down ~34% YoY and restructuring efforts underway. However, the probability of a bankruptcy filing before July 1, 2025, is extremely low because they just cleared their immediate liquidity hurdle. In March 2025, Chegg successfully paid off $358.9 million of maturing 2025 senior convertible notes in cash. They also opportunistically repurchased $56.6 million of their 2026 notes, leaving only ~$71.4 million in 2026 notes maturing in September 2026. As of early 2025, their cash position exceeded their total outstanding debt. Given that they have over a year before their next major debt maturity and still maintain positive gross margins, there is no pressing liquidity wall that would force a chapter 11 filing within the next less-than-two months. While long-term survival is questionable, a near-term filing by July 1, 2025 is highly improbable.
